crops.name: Rice

stages.name: Nursery and Seedling
key_activities: Soak and pre-germinate seeds for 24 hours before sowing.
Sow on a raised wet nursery bed; keep a thin film of water.
Apply a light dose of DAP at 7-10 days after sowing.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: DAP 100 g per 10 sq m of nursery bed.

stages.name: Transplanting and Establishment
key_activities: Transplant 20-25 day-old seedlings, 2-3 per hill.
Maintain 2-3 cm standing water for the first week.
Gap-fill any missing hills within 7 days.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Basal: 60 kg Urea + 75 kg DAP + 25 kg MOP per hectare.

stages.name: Active Tillering
key_activities: Apply first top-dress of urea at 25-30 days after transplanting.
Hand-weed or use a cono-weeder twice during this stage.
Keep water level at 3-5 cm; do not let the field dry.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Top-dress 65 kg Urea per hectare.

stages.name: Panicle Initiation and Booting
key_activities: Apply second urea top-dress at panicle initiation.
Scout fields twice a week for blast and brown plant hopper.
Maintain 5 cm water; do not drain at booting.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Top-dress 65 kg Urea + 25 kg MOP per hectare.

stages.name: Flowering and Grain Filling
key_activities: Keep a thin layer of water; avoid water stress at flowering.
Inspect for false smut and neck blast after light rain.
Stop nitrogen fertilizer; do not spray during peak flowering hours.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: No nitrogen. Foliar 1% KCl spray if leaves yellow.

stages.name: Maturity and Harvest
key_activities: Drain the field 7-10 days before expected harvest.
Harvest when 80% of grains in the panicle turn straw-yellow.
Thresh and sun-dry grain to about 14% moisture.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None at this stage.

top_diseases.name: Blast (leaf and neck)
top_diseases.symptoms: Diamond-shaped grey lesions on leaves; black rotted neck below panicle.

top_diseases.name: Sheath blight
top_diseases.symptoms: Greenish-grey oval lesions on leaf sheath near water line, spreading upward.

top_diseases.name: Bacterial leaf blight
top_diseases.symptoms: Yellow water-soaked streaks from leaf tip turning straw-coloured.

crops.harvest_window_indicators: Harvest when 80% of grains are firm and straw-yellow and lower leaves dry; grain moisture near 20-22%.
crops.storage_tip: Dry grain to 14% moisture and store in clean, sealed jute or PP bags on a wooden pallet away from walls.

crops.name: Tomato

stages.name: Nursery
key_activities: Sow treated seeds in raised beds or pro-trays under shade net.
Water lightly twice a day; avoid water-logging.
Harden seedlings by reducing water 3 days before transplanting.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Well-rotted FYM 2 kg per sq m of nursery bed.

stages.name: Transplanting and Establishment
key_activities: Transplant 25-30 day-old seedlings at 60 x 45 cm spacing.
Irrigate immediately after transplanting and again on day 3.
Apply basal dose at planting and stake or mulch the rows.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Basal: 50 kg Urea + 200 kg SSP + 40 kg MOP per hectare.

stages.name: Vegetative Growth
key_activities: Stake plants and remove side shoots below the first flower cluster.
Irrigate every 4-5 days; keep soil evenly moist.
Scout twice weekly for leaf miner and whitefly.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Top-dress 50 kg Urea per hectare 30 days after transplant.

stages.name: Flowering and Fruit Set
key_activities: Maintain steady irrigation; uneven moisture causes flower drop and cracking.
Spray 0.5% calcium nitrate if blossom-end rot symptoms appear.
Avoid heavy nitrogen; it delays flowering.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Top-dress 50 kg Urea + 30 kg MOP per hectare.

stages.name: Fruit Development
key_activities: Pick ripe fruits every 3-4 days to keep the plant productive.
Continue scouting for fruit borer; collect and destroy bored fruits.
Reduce irrigation slightly as fruits ripen to improve flavour.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Foliar 19:19:19 NPK at 5 g per litre once a week.

stages.name: Harvest
key_activities: Harvest at breaker or pink stage for distant markets, red ripe for local sale.
Pick in early morning; place fruits in shaded crates.
Grade by size and remove cracked or bruised fruits.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None.

top_diseases.name: Early blight
top_diseases.symptoms: Brown concentric-ring spots on older leaves spreading upward.

top_diseases.name: Leaf curl virus
top_diseases.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of young leaves; stunted plants. Spread by whitefly.

top_diseases.name: Bacterial wilt
top_diseases.symptoms: Sudden wilting of green plants; cut stem oozes a white bacterial stream in water.

crops.harvest_window_indicators: Start picking 65-75 days after transplanting when fruit shoulders turn from green to light pink.
crops.storage_tip: Store ripe fruit at room temperature in a single layer, shaded and ventilated, for up to 5-7 days.

crops.name: Groundnut

stages.name: Sowing and Germination
key_activities: Treat kernels with rhizobium and Trichoderma before sowing.
Sow 30 x 10 cm spacing at 4-5 cm depth in moist soil.
Apply gypsum and basal fertilizer in furrows.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Basal: 25 kg Urea + 100 kg SSP + 45 kg MOP + 400 kg gypsum per hectare.

stages.name: Vegetative Growth
key_activities: Hand-weed at 20 and 35 days after sowing.
Light earthing-up to encourage peg formation.
Irrigate at 10-12 day intervals if no rain.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: No top-dress nitrogen needed; legume fixes its own.

stages.name: Flowering and Pegging
key_activities: Do NOT disturb the soil once pegs start entering it.
Apply 200 kg gypsum per hectare on the row at peak flowering.
Ensure even soil moisture; stress now reduces pod set.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Gypsum 200 kg per hectare top-dress at flowering.

stages.name: Pod Development
key_activities: Irrigate every 8-10 days; this is the most critical water stage.
Scout for late leaf spot and rust; spray Mancozeb if 10% leaves affected.
Hand-pull weeds; do not hoe between rows.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Foliar 2% urea spray if leaves pale.

stages.name: Maturity and Harvest
key_activities: Check 4-5 sample plants; harvest when 70-75% pods show dark veins inside the shell.
Pull plants when soil is slightly moist to keep pods attached.
Sun-dry pods on a clean tarpaulin to 8-9% moisture.
common_diseases: Aflatoxin contamination if pods stay damp
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None.

top_diseases.name: Tikka leaf spot
top_diseases.symptoms: Round brown spots with yellow halo on leaves; severe defoliation.

top_diseases.name: Rust
top_diseases.symptoms: Reddish-brown powdery pustules on the underside of leaves.

top_diseases.name: Bud necrosis virus
top_diseases.symptoms: Necrotic ring spots on young leaves; bud death and bunchy top.

crops.harvest_window_indicators: Pull at 100-110 days when leaves yellow and 70%+ pod insides show dark vein pattern and firm kernels.
crops.storage_tip: Dry pods to 8% moisture and store in clean, dry bags off the floor; high moisture causes aflatoxin.

crops.name: Banana

stages.name: Planting and Establishment
key_activities: Plant disease-free tissue-culture or sword suckers in 45 x 45 x 45 cm pits.
Apply FYM and basal fertilizer in the pit before planting.
Irrigate immediately and weekly until shoots harden.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Basal per pit: 10 kg FYM + 100 g Urea + 250 g SSP + 100 g MOP.

stages.name: Vegetative Growth
key_activities: Remove unwanted suckers; keep one healthy follower per mat.
Mulch the basin with dried leaves to conserve moisture.
Apply nitrogen and potash in 4-6 split doses.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Per plant total: 200 g N + 60 g P + 300 g K, split monthly.

stages.name: Flowering and Shooting
key_activities: Stake the pseudostem to prevent lodging once the bunch emerges.
Remove the male bud (navel) one week after the last hand opens.
Cover the bunch with a perforated polythene sleeve.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Last potash split 100 g MOP per plant at shooting.

stages.name: Bunch Development
key_activities: Irrigate regularly; water stress now reduces finger size.
Inspect weekly for thrips and corm weevil.
Prop heavy bunches with bamboo to prevent breakage.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Foliar 1% MOP spray if finger filling is weak.

stages.name: Harvest
key_activities: Harvest at three-quarter maturity (fingers angular but rounding).
Cut the bunch carefully; do not let it fall on the ground.
De-hand only after 24 hours of latex drainage.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None.

top_diseases.name: Panama wilt (Fusarium)
top_diseases.symptoms: Yellowing and collapse of older leaves around pseudostem; reddish-brown vascular streaks inside corm.

top_diseases.name: Sigatoka leaf spot
top_diseases.symptoms: Yellow streaks turning to brown elliptical spots with grey centres on leaves.

top_diseases.name: Bunchy top virus
top_diseases.symptoms: Narrow upright bunched leaves at top; dark green dot-dash streaks on midrib. Spread by aphid.

crops.harvest_window_indicators: Harvest when fingers are 75% mature, slightly angular, and 90-110 days after shooting depending on variety.
crops.storage_tip: Keep harvested bunches in a cool, shaded, well-ventilated area; do not stack tightly. Ripen at 15-18 C.

crops.name: Onion

stages.name: Nursery
key_activities: Sow seeds on raised beds; cover lightly with sieved FYM.
Water with a fine rose-can twice a day in summer.
Drench beds with copper oxychloride if damping-off appears.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: FYM 2 kg + 20 g DAP per sq m of nursery bed.

stages.name: Transplanting and Establishment
key_activities: Transplant 40-45 day-old seedlings at 15 x 10 cm spacing.
Irrigate immediately and again on day 3.
Apply basal fertilizer in the furrow before planting.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Basal: 30 kg Urea + 125 kg SSP + 65 kg MOP per hectare.

stages.name: Vegetative Growth
key_activities: Hand-weed twice; do not hoe deep, roots are shallow.
Irrigate every 7-10 days, keep soil evenly moist.
Scout weekly for thrips; treat early.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Top-dress 30 kg Urea per hectare at 30 DAT.

stages.name: Bulb Development
key_activities: Stop nitrogen; excess N delays bulb formation and softens skin.
Reduce irrigation frequency to harden the bulbs.
Spray Mancozeb if purple blotch lesions appear.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Foliar 1% potassium sulphate to improve skin colour.

stages.name: Maturity and Harvest
key_activities: Stop irrigation 10-15 days before harvest.
Lift bulbs when 50-70% of tops fall over and necks soften.
Cure bulbs in shade for 7-10 days before storage.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None.

top_diseases.name: Purple blotch
top_diseases.symptoms: Small white sunken spots on leaves enlarging to purple zonate lesions; tips die back.

top_diseases.name: Thrips damage
top_diseases.symptoms: Silvery-white streaks and curled leaves; tiny yellow insects inside leaf folds.

top_diseases.name: Basal rot
top_diseases.symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of leaves; soft rotted base with white fungal growth on the basal plate.

crops.harvest_window_indicators: Harvest when 50-70% of tops have fallen over, necks are soft, and outer scales are dry and coloured.
crops.storage_tip: After 7-10 days of shade curing, store bulbs in well-ventilated bamboo crates in a cool dry place; avoid stacking over 60 cm.

crops.name: Sugarcane

stages.name: Planting and Germination
key_activities: Use 3-budded setts from healthy 9-10 month-old cane.
Treat setts with carbendazim and hot-water if available.
Plant in furrows 90 cm apart; cover with 3-5 cm soil.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Basal: 75 kg Urea + 250 kg SSP + 120 kg MOP per hectare.

stages.name: Tillering
key_activities: First top-dress nitrogen at 45 days; second at 90 days.
Hand-weed or use pre-emergence herbicide; keep field weed-free.
Irrigate every 10-12 days in dry season.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Two splits of 75 kg Urea per hectare at 45 and 90 days.

stages.name: Grand Growth
key_activities: Earth-up rows at 120 and 150 days to support stalks.
Tie 4-5 canes from adjacent rows to prevent lodging.
Maintain regular irrigation; this stage forms most of the cane weight.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: Final 75 kg Urea + 60 kg MOP per hectare top-dress at 120 days.

stages.name: Maturation and Ripening
key_activities: Reduce irrigation gradually 30 days before harvest to raise sugar content.
Strip dry lower leaves to improve airflow and reduce rats.
Test brix; harvest when juice brix reaches 18-20%.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None. Optional foliar 1% KCl if leaves yellow.

stages.name: Harvest
key_activities: Cut canes at ground level; trim tops and tie in bundles.
Transport to mill within 24 hours of cutting to avoid sucrose loss.
Plan ratoon management within a week of harvest.
stages.recommended_fertilizer: None for plant crop; ratoon needs 25% extra N.

top_diseases.name: Red rot
top_diseases.symptoms: Reddened internal pith with cross-wise white patches; sour smell; leaves dry from top.

top_diseases.name: Smut
top_diseases.symptoms: Long black whip-like structure emerging from the top of the shoot; thin sickly tillers.

top_diseases.name: Early shoot borer
top_diseases.symptoms: Dead heart in young shoots; central whorl dries while outer leaves stay green.

crops.harvest_window_indicators: Harvest at 10-12 months when juice brix is 18-20%, lower leaves dry, and stalks sound metallic when tapped.
crops.storage_tip: Sugarcane does not store well after cutting; crush within 24-48 hours. For seed setts, keep bundles upright in shade and moisten lightly.
